intro: Rhaponticoides tamanianae, also known as the Armenian stonecrop, is a perennial succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, gastrointestinal problems, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhaponticoides tamanianae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
